title: anthemis cotula subsp. psorosperma
intro: Anthemis cotula subsp. psorosperma, commonly known as Stinking Chamomile or Scentless Mayweed, is a plant species within the daisy family, characterized by its small, white flowers and bitter, unpleasant odor. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been utilized to treat a variety of health issues, including skin conditions and digestive problems. The health benefits of Anthemis cotula subsp. psorospermer include its ability to soothe and calm the skin, reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, as well as its potential to alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution due to its potential allergenic and skin-irritating effects.
